title: Chinese models Kimi K3 and DeepSeek challenge Western leadership in artificial intelligence
summary: Advances in Chinese AI models cause an earthquake in global markets and revive the debate on the technological gap with the United States.

title: Bethesda confirms Fallout 5, remasters of Fallout 3 and New Vegas, and news on The Elder Scrolls VI
summary: The studio announces the future of its main sagas, but the union warns that the news should not hide the massive layoffs in the company.

title: Apple surpasses Nvidia in market value driven by AI while the graphics industry accelerates its investments
summary: Apple briefly becomes the most valuable company in the world, while Nvidia updates its software and delays the launch of the RTX 50 SUPER.

title: Sony faces criticism over game preservation, the end of physical discs and the potential high price of the PS6
summary: Various voices warn about the future of physical media, digital preservation of video games and the possible cost of Sony's next console.

title: Multiple security flaws in surveillance cameras and robot vacuum cleaners expose user privacy
summary: Investigations reveal vulnerabilities in devices from Flock, TP-Link and Shark that allow spying and tracking without consent.

title: Several alleged cybercriminals detained linked to malware hidden in Steam games and the REvil ransomware
summary: The FBI arrests a man for stealing cryptocurrencies through Steam games with malware, while Armenia detains a Russian tourist wanted for the REvil case.

title: New scandals surround Trump: teleprompter, betting, stocks and privileged access to Truth Social
summary: A teleprompter operator wins $100,000 betting on speeches, Trump promotes stocks on Truth Social and his family company charges millions for fast access.

title: LG installs advertising software on monitors and TVs through Windows Update without user permission
summary: Users report that LG monitors install adware and demand to warn visitors about AI recording to comply with wiretapping laws.

title: Kaiser nurses denounce that artificial intelligence and workplace surveillance worsen their work and patient care
summary: A group of nurses at Kaiser hospital say that AI tools and constant monitoring are negatively affecting the quality of patient care.

title: Apple and Google receive orders to remove AI nudity apps from their app stores
summary: Both the city of San Francisco and a court order demand Apple and Google remove 'nudify' apps that generate fake intimate images without consent.

title: AMD launches Ryzen 7 7700X3D in an exclusive bundle with motherboard and memory on Newegg
summary: The new AMD Ryzen 7 7700X3D processor is offered in a pack with B650E board and 16GB DDR5 for $450 in the United States.

title: Mario Kart Wii comes to PC reconverted with AI, with 4K resolution and unlimited frame rate
summary: A fan project achieves the first static recompilation of a Wii game, allowing players to play Mario Kart Wii on PC with over 200 tracks and improved graphics.

title: A bug in AWS billing showed charges of $1.7 billion to some customers
summary: Amazon corrects an error in its billing system that generated incorrect charge estimates worth billions of dollars for several AWS users.

title: Australia approves a regulatory framework that forces AI data centers to generate their own electricity
summary: The Australian Prime Minister announces that artificial intelligence data centers must produce as much energy as they consume, also guaranteeing water efficiency.

title: The robotics industry stirs: Zoox recalls robotaxis, Agility sets up in Tesla's backyard and military robots appear
summary: A robotaxi startup recalls vehicles for confusing smoke, Agility Robotics opens factory in California and a humanoid company backed by Eric Trump prepares for war.

title: SoftBank CEO calls AI bubble critics 'too stupid to understand what's happening'
summary: Masayoshi Son defends that artificial intelligence is not a bubble and harshly criticizes those who doubt the potential of the technology.

title: Tech executives travel with armed bodyguards amid growing social rejection of artificial intelligence
summary: The increase in criticism and backlash against AI leads top executives of technology companies to step up personal security measures.

title: Poland approves prison sentences for streamers who profit from violence and humiliation live
summary: The Polish government bans so-called 'trash streams' and establishes prison sentences for those who generate income through violent or humiliating content.

title: SpaceX shares plummet and lose a trillion dollars in value after Starship launch abort
summary: Elon Musk's space company suffers a historic stock market fall after the Starship rocket test was aborted at the last second.

title: Apple's antitrust lawsuit puts OpenAI's IPO plans at risk
summary: The lawsuit filed against Apple by the US Department of Justice could affect the agreement between Apple and OpenAI, threatening the valuation and initial public offering of the AI company.
